headlines.title: U.S. strikes two locations in Iran near Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: The U.S. military hit two locations in Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, according to a U.S. official. NBC News’ Matt Bradley explains what this means for the war with Iran.

headlines.title: Source: DOJ investigating suspicious oil trades timed around Iran war
headlines.description: The Justice Department is investigating a series of oil market trades that right before important moves related to the war with Iran. At least four trades worth $2.6 billion are being examined. NBC News’ Allie Canal explains the timing of the bets.

headlines.title: Iran-backed groups targeted U.S. facilities in Iraq 600 times, U.S. official says
headlines.description: The U.S. is urging the government to break with Tehran-backed militia groups following more than 600 attacks against U.S. diplomatic facilities.

headlines.title: Hard-right party routs Starmer’s Labour in U.K. elections as Trump ally hails ‘historic shift’
headlines.description: Early results Friday from nationwide elections in Britain suggested a historic drubbing for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party and sweeping gains for hard-right Reform U.K., led by Trump ally Nigel Farage.

headlines.title: April jobs report expected to show gains despite Iran war pressure
headlines.description: The April jobs coming Friday morning is expected to show a resilient U.S. labor market despite the global energy shock from the U.S. war with Iran.

headlines.title: Redistricting pits California Republican incumbents against each other in a fight for survival
headlines.description: Facing a tough election challenge is a cost of doing business for members of Congress.

headlines.title: Multiple passengers from cruise ship back in U.S.
headlines.description: Five states - Texas, Georgia, Virginia, Arizona, and California - confirm passengers who had left the cruise ship before the Hantavirus outbreak was known about, are back home. None are showing symptoms. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports on what steps are being taken to mitigate the risk of any spread of the virus.

headlines.title: Christian missionary hailed as ‘holiest man alive’ pleads guilty to abusing boys in Texas
headlines.description: Daniel Savala, who preached for decades in an Assemblies of God-run college ministry, was sentenced to 30 years in prison without the possibility of parole.

headlines.title: Is blue dot fever the cause of tour cancellations?
headlines.description: Blue dot fever is a term coined by the internet and tabloid newspapers for when concerts are canceled due to low ticket sales. Artists like Post Malone, Meghan Trainor and the Pussycat Dolls are just some to pull the plug on live performances over prices.

headlines.title: K-pop supergroup BTS greet fans in Mexico City
headlines.description: K-pop supergroup BTS greet fans in Mexico City

headlines.title: Alligator appears to knock on Florida front door
headlines.description: Alligator appears to knock on Florida front door

headlines.title: Fire truck crash leaves two firefighters in hospital
headlines.description: Fire truck crash leaves two firefighters in hospital
